meta.title: Discovery V8 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Land Rover Discovery V8 engine — 3.5, 3.9 and 4.0. D1 and D2 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

@graph.name: Land Rover Discovery Rover V8 Engine Era Map
Land Rover Discovery Rover V8 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Rover V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Rover V8 typically costs £1,800–£4,000 supply only depending on displacement (3.5, 3.9 or 4.0). Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Discovery Rover V8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the displacement. The 3.5 is widely considered the most reliable; the 3.9 is decent but has head‑gasket and liner issues; the 4.0 has a well‑documented liner‑slip and oil‑pump problem, earning a lower rating. See the Era Map above for specifics.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery Rover V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cylinder liner slippage (especially on 4.0 and 3.9), oil pump failure (4.0), and head gasket failure (3.9/4.0). The 3.5 is largely exempt from these issues and is the most robust choice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Rover V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a clean D2 worth £3,000–£6,000, a £2,200–£4,000 recon is a significant but often worthwhile investment if the rest of the vehicle is solid. For a D1 with a 3.5, replacement is usually viable given the classic status. Always assess the chassis and body condition first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery's engine displacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is stamped on the block near the distributor or on the VIN plate. For D1, it's either 3.5 or 3.9; for D2, it's 4.0. Check the service book or use our free VIN lookup.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the Rover V8's cylinder liners, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: If caught early, a top‑hat liner installation can save the block, costing £1,500–£3,000. If the liner has dropped and cracked the block, a replacement engine is the only realistic fix.

eraMap.title: The DISCOVERY ROVER V8 THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The classic 3.5 and 3.9 – robust but watch for liner slip and head gaskets on the 3.9.
The 4.0 is prone to liner failure and top‑end oil starvation – buy with a known service history.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery Rover V8 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Most owners — best value for the 3.5/3.9
Long‑term classic ownership; full rebuild with upgraded components
No longer available from Land Rover

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery V8 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery Rover V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: If caught before block damage; otherwise replacement is cheaper
Preventative — replace the pump and pick‑up before seizure
Upgrade to MLS gaskets for a permanent fix
Once bearings are gone, the block is often scrap
Simple fix; parts are widely available

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with documented oil changes — the 4.0 is unforgiving of neglect
* Proven cylinder liner work (top‑hat liners fitted) on a 4.0 or 3.9
* Oil pressure gauge reading at least 2.5 bar when hot at 2,000 rpm
* A 3.5‑litre car — the most robust of the three displacements
avoidIf: * 4.0 with no record of liner work or oil pump replacement
* 3.9 or 4.0 showing signs of coolant loss with no visible leak
* Any V8 with a rattling top end on start‑up (worn timing gears or oil starvation)
* Engine warning light or misfire that hasn't been diagnosed

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Rover V8 is a characterful and repairable engine, but the 3.9 and 4.0 have well‑known flaws that can turn a cheap Discovery into a money pit. The 3.5 is the safest bet, while the 4.0 demands a documented history of liner and oil‑pump work. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with upgraded liners and a new oil pump is the only sensible choice.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery Rover V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle or tick from the top end (timing chain or oil starvation)
* Service History — Including which engine displacement (3.5/3.9/4.0) — see the Era Map above
* Coolant Level — Is the coolant low with no visible leak? Liner slip or head gasket risk
* Oil Pressure — Check the gauge: does it drop below 1 bar when hot at idle?
* Exhaust Smoke — White smoke indicates coolant burning; blue smoke is oil consumption
* Oil Consumption — Rover V8s do use some oil, but more than 1L per 1,000 miles is a red flag
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved misfire or lambda issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures; often point to fuelling or ignition problems

faq.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited‑mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
